mandate: Inverted lens: hunt realized own-conduct improvements only. No announcements, pledges, or PR.
note: All 1,260 entities were covered via sector-sweep batch queries; only entities with actual positive signals appear in entity_positive_records. All others are covered by coverage_summary.

upgrade_candidates.name: Venezuela
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: June 9, 2026: 54 military detainees confirmed released as political prisoners, including 3 women, verified by Coalición por los Derechos Humanos y la Democracia and Foro Penal. This is the most recent tranche of a broader wave: 621 political prisoners confirmed released since Jan 8 when the interim Delcy Rodriguez government began releases following Maduro's capture on Jan 3. June 18: democratic transition talks held between National Assembly and opposition representatives. Wikipedia article '2026 political prisoner release in Venezuela' documents the full wave.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: REALIZED — releases confirmed by NGOs with named beneficiaries and dates. The 54 June 9 releases are within the 30-day window. The larger 621 figure spans back to January 2026.
upgrade_candidates.attribution_check: Actions attributable to Venezuela's interim government (Delcy Rodriguez administration). The US applied pressure, but releases were executed by Venezuelan authorities.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: moderate — political prisoner releases are material to Dimension 3 (Reduction of Suffering / Liberty). However: authoritarian apparatus intact, 400+ prisoners remain, democratic transition stalled, Foro Penal notes no systemic dismantling of repressive structures. Net change likely +5 to +8 points on composite, not a band change.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: ASSESS — material change in political prisoner numbers. Attribution confirmed. Some realized releases within 30-day window. Temper by: 400+ remain, no structural dismantling.

upgrade_candidates.name: Anthropic
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: June 10, 2026: Anthropic published its Advanced AI Framework — binding regulatory proposals including mandatory third-party safety testing, government authority to block failed deployments, and a companion Economic Policy Framework with a $350M pledge for AI-driven labor transition. Alongside CEO Dario Amodei's essay 'Policy on the AI Exponential,' this represents the most operationalized safety governance publication from any AI lab in the 30-day window. The Advanced AI Framework PDF is publicly hosted on Anthropic's CDN. G7 Évian participation (June 17-21): Dario Amodei joined G7 leaders for AI safety working lunch. Context note: The nightly scan also documents the Fable/Mythos export control situation, which is a negative governance signal, but it is attributable to US government action, not Anthropic's own conduct.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: REALIZED — policy framework published (a concrete governance action). The $350M economic pledge is announced/not yet disbursed. The mandatory testing framework is a regulatory proposal, not yet law — but the publication itself is a realized transparency/governance action.
upgrade_candidates.attribution_check: Fully attributable to Anthropic's own conduct.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: small-to-moderate — the Advanced AI Framework advances Dimension 7 (Governance and Transparency). Composite is at 60.0, precisely at the functional/established boundary. A +3 to +5 could push into 'established' band. Offset: export control situation (nightly scan) is a negative pressure.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: ASSESS — governance transparency action realized within window. Balance against export control negative signal from nightly scan.

upgrade_candidates.name: OpenAI
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: May 28, 2026: OpenAI published its Frontier Governance Framework — a public document mapping internal safety and security practices to California SB 53 (Transparency in Frontier AI Act) and the EU AI Act's Code of Practice for General Purpose AI Models. Requirements include: standing Safety Advisory Group, third-party red-teaming, maintained AI Safety Incident Response Plan, ISO and SOC 2 certifications, encrypted weight storage with multi-party controls, six-monthly model reporting cadence, and board committee with systemic-risk oversight. This is a concrete governance transparency action realized within the window. Counter-signals: State AGs investigation launched June 13 (negative); 'safely' removed from mission statement (negative); IPO filing underway. Net governance signal is mixed but the Frontier Governance Framework publication is a positive realized action.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: REALIZED — framework published as a public document. Governance structures described are stated as 'in place.'
upgrade_candidates.estimated_score_direction: MIXED — positive governance transparency, offset by concurrent negative signals. Net: likely no composite change, or minimal +1 to +2.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: REVIEW — positive governance publication within window, but concurrent negative signals. Do not upgrade without full assessment balancing both.

upgrade_candidates.name: United Kingdom
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: Employment Rights Act 2025 (Royal Assent December 18, 2025) implementation is rolling out across 2026. The Act — described as 'the most significant overhaul of employment law in a generation' — makes statutory sick pay a day-one right, paternity leave a day-one right, and simplifies trade union recognition. The Fair Work Agency launched April 7, 2026 as a single regulator for key employment rights. Within the 30-day window (May 28-June 27): Consultations active on NDAs in harassment cases (open until July 8) and zero-hours contracts (open until August 25). Certain phased provisions are entering force. Attribution: UK government's own enacted legislation.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: PARTIALLY REALIZED — core legislation enacted December 2025, implementation phased through 2026-2027. Fair Work Agency launch (April 2026) is realized but outside window. Within-window: consultations and further phased provisions — more 'process' than 'enacted.'
upgrade_candidates.within_30d_window: partial — framework enacted before window; phased implementation ongoing within window
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: The Employment Rights Act is a major structural labor reform that warrants score reassessment. However, it was enacted December 2025 (outside window). The question for scoring is whether it has been captured in prior assessments. If not, this is a material gap. Est. +3 to +5 on labor/worker welfare dimensions.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: REVIEW — Employment Rights Act is a significant worker welfare reform. Confirm it was captured in the April 2026 assessment. If not, flag for reassessment.

upgrade_candidates.name: Canada
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: Spring Economic Update 2026 (Carney government): $4B+ pledged for Indigenous healthcare, culturally relevant education, and nutritious food access; $4.6B for clean drinking water in First Nations communities. First Indigenous person (Mandy Gull-Masty, former Grand Chief of Grand Council of Crees) appointed as Minister of Indigenous Services. National Council for Reconciliation established March 2026. June 21, 2026: PM Carney issued statement on National Indigenous Peoples Day. These represent a cluster of concrete policy actions on Indigenous rights and reconciliation within or near the 30-day window.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: MIXED — Spring Economic Update pledges are announced (funds not yet disbursed); ministerial appointment is realized; Council establishment is realized.
upgrade_candidates.within_30d_window: partially — Spring Economic Update typically released May-June; ministerial appointment April 2026; National Council March 2026. Need precise Spring Update date to confirm window.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: Canada at 79.6 is high-scoring. Indigenous reconciliation progress is meaningful on Dimension 4 (Equity/Access) and Dimension 6 (Structural Change). Est. +2 to +4.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: REVIEW — Indigenous funding pledges and Council establishment are positive. Confirm Spring Economic Update timing. Attribution confirmed to Canadian government.

upgrade_candidates.name: Ukraine
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: May 9-11, 2026: US-brokered three-day ceasefire agreed (within 30-day window). May 15, 2026: Ukraine and Russia exchanged 205 POWs each — first phase of 1,000-for-1,000 prisoner exchange. Zelenskyy confirmed this was the primary Ukrainian condition for the ceasefire. The POW exchange is a realized reduction in suffering for 205 individuals (and implicitly the families of 205 Russian prisoners returned to Russia). The ceasefire itself held for three days but then collapsed with renewed strikes.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: REALIZED — 205 POW releases confirmed by both sides on May 15. The ceasefire itself was implemented but violated. Net: prisoners were actually released.
upgrade_candidates.attribution_check: Ukraine agreed to ceasefire terms and executed the POW release. Credit attributable to Ukraine (and Russia, which is separately assessed).
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: POW releases are significant for those 205 individuals but do not substantially change Ukraine's aggregate score while the war continues and civilian casualties mount. Est. +1 to +2.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: NOTE — 205 POW releases realized, within window. Small positive signal on Dimension 3 (prisoner welfare). Does not change overall war-driven scoring trajectory.

upgrade_candidates.name: Colombia
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: April 6, 2026: Colombian President Petro announced Comuneros del Sur Front (ELN dissident faction, ~200-300 fighters, Nariño Department) agreed to progressive disarmament and replacement of 5,000 hectares of coca. Disarmament deadline: June 27, 2026 (scan date). Confirmed actions: group handed over war material (grenades, explosives, devices) at ceremony in Pasto in April-June 2026. This is the first concrete ELN-aligned disarmament under Total Peace. Context: ELN national peace talks cancelled; this is a sub-group action. Impact described as 'symbolic more than substantive' by InSight Crime — a small group in a crowded armed landscape.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: REALIZED (partial) — progressive disarmament underway and confirmed by government ceremony and war material handover. June 27 deadline is the scan date; final status unconfirmed.
upgrade_candidates.attribution_check: Colombia government and Comuneros del Sur joint action.
upgrade_candidates.within_30d_window: PARTIAL — disarmament process spans April-June 2026; the June 27 deadline falls within the window. Material handover events confirmed within window period.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: Small group, limited territory, symbolic significance exceeds operational impact. Est. +1 to +2 on peace/security dimensions.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: NOTE — small realized disarmament step. Do not overweight; ELN national talks cancelled and other armed groups expanding.

upgrade_candidates.name: Sweden
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: June 7, 2026: Sweden's implementing legislation for the EU Gender Pay Transparency Directive came into force. The legislation requires employers to provide salary information to job applicants, allows employee access to pay data, and imposes gender pay gap reporting obligations on larger companies. Sweden joins EU member states meeting the June 7, 2026 directive transposition deadline. Additionally, from June 1, 2026 other regulatory changes took effect in Sweden (food requirement for alcohol licenses abolished). From July 1: VAT reduction on cultural events.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: REALIZED — legislation came into force June 7, 2026. This is an enacted legal right for workers.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: Sweden already at 97.2 (exemplary). Pay transparency is a genuine positive but marginal at this score level. Est. +0 to +1. Confirms continued improvement trajectory.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: NOTE — confirmed within-window enacted reform. For reassessment purposes, marginal at this score level.

upgrade_candidates.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: June 24, 2026: Sixth Joint Oversight Committee meeting (London) between DRC, Rwanda, US, Qatar, Togo, and African Union Commission. Progress toward signing of Protocol on Humanitarian Access and Judicial Protection, and MOU with ICGLR to operationalize Expanded Joint Verification Mechanism Plus. Between March-June 2026: MONUSCO facilitated repatriation of 156 former Rwandan combatants and 163 dependents associated with FDLR and other foreign armed groups. May 2026: M23 withdrew from several towns (Sange, north of Uvira). However: violence continues, Ebola outbreak ongoing, 53.3% funding gap in humanitarian plan. The positive signals are procedural progress and limited combatant repatriation — not sustained peace.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: PARTIAL — JOC meeting is realized (diplomacy); 156 combatant repatriations are realized; M23 withdrawal from some towns realized. Protocol on Humanitarian Access is still 'in progress' as of June 24.
upgrade_candidates.attribution_check: Multi-party peace process; not attributable to DRC government alone.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: At 2.3, any improvement is notable, but peace implementation has fundamentally stalled on the major objectives (M23 withdrawal, ceasefire holding). Process signals do not justify a score change without more material improvement. Est. 0 to +1.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: NOTE — procedural progress and limited combatant repatriations realized in window. Insufficient for score upgrade without sustained ceasefire.

upgrade_candidates.name: Google DeepMind
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: June 17-21, 2026: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis participated in G7 Évian AI safety working lunch, committing to voluntary safety-by-design for minors, age-appropriate model behavior, and watermarking of synthetic content. April 17, 2026 (just outside window): Google DeepMind published strengthened Frontier Safety Framework v3, adding Tracked Capability Levels (TCLs) to identify emerging risks. Google's 2026 Responsible AI Progress Report was released February 2026 (outside window). Governing AI at Scale: Google's 2026 Responsibility Plan published. EU AI Act compliance preparation underway.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: G7 commitments are voluntary/non-binding pledges. FSF v3 is a realized governance document (April, outside window). G7 participation is within window.
upgrade_candidates.within_30d_window: Partial — G7 participation within window; FSF update outside window.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: Non-binding G7 pledges have limited weight under 'realized own-conduct' standard. The FSF v3 (April) is more meaningful but outside window. Recommend capturing FSF v3 in next full reassessment.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: NOTE — G7 participation is a moderate signal. Primary upgrade evidence (FSF v3) is outside the 30-day window.

upgrade_candidates.name: Meta
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: Meta published its H1 2026 Integrity Reports (semiannual transparency package), marking a shift to biannual reporting aligned with EU Digital Services Act requirements. The package includes H2 2025 Community Standards Enforcement Report, Q4 2025 Widely Viewed Content Report, and DSA Very Large Online Platforms Transparency Reports. Additionally: Meta updated its original Frontier AI Framework to the Advanced AI Scaling Framework, introducing new Safety and Preparedness Reports that disclose risk assessments, evaluation results, and deployment decision rationale. 80% of Oversight Board's 326 recommendations committed to. Community Alignment program piloted (diverse user preferences in model development).
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: REALIZED — Integrity Reports published; Advanced AI Scaling Framework published; 80% commitment documented. Community Alignment: pilot status (announced, not yet at scale).
upgrade_candidates.within_30d_window: H1 2026 Integrity Reports likely published June 2026 (covers H1); need precise date. Advanced AI Scaling Framework — date unclear from sources but referenced as 2026.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: Transparency reports and governance frameworks represent meaningful positive governance signals. Offset by Zuckerberg AI safety case and other negative signals from prior scans.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: NOTE — transparency reports are positive governance signals. Full assessment needed to net against negative prior signals.

upgrade_candidates.name: Lebanon
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: June 26, 2026: US-brokered trilateral ceasefire framework signed by Israel, Lebanon, and US. US Secretary of State Rubio announced $100M in humanitarian assistance for Lebanon. Framework creates pilot zones where Israeli forces withdraw as Lebanese army takes sovereign control. However: Hezbollah rejected the deal June 27, calling it 'humiliation and disgrace.' Fighting continues. The $100M humanitarian aid announcement is the most concrete positive signal — if disbursed, it reduces suffering directly. The Lebanese Armed Forces assuming control in pilot zones, if implemented, would reduce non-state actor violence.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: ANNOUNCED — ceasefire framework signed but Hezbollah rejection makes implementation uncertain. $100M humanitarian aid announced (not yet disbursed). Framework is a diplomatic commitment, not yet realized.
upgrade_candidates.attribution_check: Lebanon's government signed; Lebanese Armed Forces deployment is Lebanon's own action (if implemented). $100M is US government commitment.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_score_direction: STABLE — too early to score improvement given Hezbollah rejection
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: If ceasefire holds and Lebanese Armed Forces deploy: moderate positive. If Hezbollah rejection leads to continued fighting: no improvement. Do not adjust score pending implementation.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: WATCH — ceasefire rejected by Hezbollah on scan date. Do not credit score improvement. Re-flag if LAF pilot zone deployment is confirmed in 72 hours.

upgrade_candidates.name: Syria
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: Post-Assad transitional government has been operational since December 2024. Within the 30-day window (May 28-June 27, 2026): Transitional Justice Commission (established under Decree No. 20 from May 2025) continues meeting with civil society, diaspora, and foreign governments — implementing its mandate. 581,000 Syrian refugees have returned since Assad's fall (ongoing, within window). EU announced €175M financial package for transitional justice, accountability, and human rights programs (confirmed in June 2026 context). Constitutional Declaration (March 2025) guarantees rights to equality, expression, press freedom. HTS has not used lethal force against protesters (a contrast with Assad era). Context: arbitrary detentions by transitional authorities, torture reports, deaths in custody remain serious concerns.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: PARTIAL — refugee returns are ongoing and realized. Transitional Justice Commission is operational. EU funding is announced. Arbitrary detentions continue as a counter-signal.
upgrade_candidates.attribution_check: Syrian transitional government's own actions. EU funding is external.
upgrade_candidates.within_30d_window: Ongoing processes within window; no single defining event on a specific June date.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: Syria at 0 by definition has room to move up. The transitional period is showing genuine improvements vs. Assad era — but arbitrary detentions and incomplete accountability mean this is far from stable. Score reassessment needed to establish a post-transition baseline. Previous assessment was June 5, 2026; any new evidence since then is within window.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: ASSESS — transitional justice commission operational, refugees returning, expanded freedoms. Material change from Assad-era baseline. Full assessment warranted to establish new composite.

upgrade_candidates.name: South Korea
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: Post-Yoon democratic restoration process is underway. New president Lee Jae-myung (elected June 3, 2025) declared 'war on industrial accidents' targeting OECD-average fatality rate. Parliament enacted safeguards for future martial law declarations and legislative protections. Constitutional Court upheld Yoon's removal (April 4, 2025). Within the 30-day window (June 2026): Lee appointed new senior aides to advance prosecutorial reform agenda (June 21, 2026). Implementation of democratic reform agenda ongoing. South Korea's democratic resilience in response to December 2024 martial law crisis is a model — parliament acted within hours, courts upheld impeachment.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: REALIZED (prior period) — impeachment upheld, new president installed, legislative safeguards enacted. Most realizations occurred before the 30-day window. Within window: reform implementation signals.
upgrade_candidates.attribution_check: South Korean government, parliament, and constitutional court — own-conduct institutional actions.
upgrade_candidates.within_30d_window: Partially — reform implementation ongoing; specific June 2026 actions are administrative.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: Democratic resilience and new reform agenda represent genuine positive change. The martial law crisis recovery and institutional safeguard enactment are material. Last assessment May 2, 2026 — any new evidence since then in window. Est. +3 to +6 on governance/accountability dimensions.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: REVIEW — democratic restoration is a significant positive structural change. Confirm what was captured in May 2026 assessment. If institutional safeguards not yet scored, this warrants reassessment.

upgrade_candidates.name: Amazon
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: June 4, 2026: NLRB general counsel defended Amazon joint-employer settlement before House subcommittee. The settlement (reached in late May/early June 2026) provides back pay to union-represented delivery drivers at Battle Tested Strategies (Southern California) while allowing Amazon to avoid joint-employer classification. March 31, 2026 (context, outside window): Amazon settled Teamsters retaliation charges, restoring docked unpaid time off and posting workers' rights notices across 1,300 facilities nationwide. April 2026 (outside window): JFK8 NLRB bargaining order issued. Within window: June settlement defense testimony; June 4 NLRB hearing. The March settlement's IMPLEMENTATION — including posting of notices at 1,300 facilities — likely extends into the window.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: PARTIAL — March settlement (outside window) is realized; notices posted at 1,300 facilities = within window. June back-pay settlement is within window and realized.
upgrade_candidates.attribution_check: Amazon's own compliance with NLRB settlements.
upgrade_candidates.within_30d_window: Partial — implementation of prior settlements may fall within window.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: Labor settlements represent accountability delivered — positive signal. Back-pay to workers is concrete. However, Amazon's overall labor conduct score should be assessed holistically.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: NOTE — labor accountability delivered (back pay to workers, rights notices posted). Modest positive.

upgrade_candidates.name: Germany
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: Hospital reform implementing as of January 1, 2026 with €50B Hospital Transformation Fund for care quality improvement through 2035. Nursing professionals granted independence to carry out specific medical tasks (wound care, blood sampling, diabetes care) — expanding care capacity without requiring physician oversight for routine tasks. However: Germany agreed April 2026 to €38.3B in welfare cuts by 2030 (CDU/SPD coalition). Migration law tightened. These are mixed signals — healthcare quality up, welfare spending down.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: REALIZED — hospital reform active from January 1, 2026; nursing task delegation enacted.
upgrade_candidates.within_30d_window: Implementation ongoing within window; specific legislative trigger was January 2026.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_score_direction: STABLE (mixed — healthcare quality positive, welfare cuts negative)
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: At 90.9, Germany is already high-scoring. Healthcare reforms are incremental improvements. Welfare cuts are a negative counter-signal. Net: likely stable.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: NOTE — healthcare quality reform enacted. Counter-balanced by welfare spending cuts. Net signal ambiguous.

upgrade_candidates.name: Iran
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: June 17, 2026: US and Iran signed MOU extending ceasefire 60 days and establishing framework for Strait of Hormuz reopening. This directly reduces active warfare and civilian/military casualties from the US-Iran conflict. HOWEVER: Implementation is fragile — IRGC struck a Singapore ship June 26 (same day as Lebanon ceasefire announcement). Both sides trading ceasefire violation accusations. Domestic repression: Iran executed at least 134 prisoners in May-June 2026 (one execution every 3 hours at peak). No political prisoner releases. No domestic reforms. The MOU is a CONFLICT-DIMENSION positive signal only; domestic human rights are worsening.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: Ceasefire MOU signed (realized as a formal agreement). Actual implementation fragile with violations. Strait re-opening framework agreed but not yet fully operational.
upgrade_candidates.attribution_check: Iran's government signed the MOU — their own conduct in committing to ceasefire. Violations are also Iran's own conduct (IRGC strikes).
upgrade_candidates.estimated_score_direction: STABLE — conflict dimension marginal positive; domestic dimension worsening. Net: no composite improvement warranted.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: NOTE — MOU signed (positive for war cessation dimension) but domestic executions and repression accelerating. Do not score upgrade; conflicted signals.

upgrade_candidates.name: Mistral AI
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: June 17-21, 2026: Mistral AI CEO participated in G7 Évian summit AI safety working lunch, committing to voluntary G7 AI safety pledges (child safety by design, frontier risk in cyber/bio domains, synthetic content watermarking). Mistral is a signatory of frontier AI safety commitments from AI Seoul Summit. Mistral published safety governance policy for its 12 frontier AI models. Singapore partnership with HTX for public safety AI announced within the period.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: G7 commitments are voluntary/non-binding. Safety policy publication is realized governance documentation.
upgrade_candidates.estimated_magnitude: Non-binding pledges have limited weight. Safety policy publication is more meaningful as governance documentation.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: NOTE — G7 participation is a positive governance signal. Weight modestly.

upgrade_candidates.name: United States
upgrade_candidates.evidence_summary: June 2, 2026: White House EO 'Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security' — establishes voluntary framework for frontier AI developers to engage with government pre-deployment for cybersecurity review. June 9, 2026: House passed Faster Labor Contracts Act (H.R. 5408) requiring mandatory collective bargaining timelines (10 days to begin, 90 days to reach agreement, then FMCS mediation and binding arbitration). However: House passage does not guarantee Senate passage or enactment. US also applied USAID cuts affecting humanitarian operations globally (negative). Venezuela intervention (positive for regional political prisoners). US-Iran MOU (positive for conflict). Multiple negative signals continue: rollback of DEI, NLRB weakening under Trump admin, Apple/union discrimination. Net: mixed signals, House labor bill is most positive.
upgrade_candidates.realized_vs_announced: House labor bill PASSED but not yet signed into law. White House AI EO is signed (realized). Venezuela intervention effects are realized (Maduro removed, prisoners released — but attributable to Venezuela's own choices post-intervention).
upgrade_candidates.estimated_score_direction: STABLE — positive AI governance and labor bill offset by many negative signals ongoing. No change warranted pending Senate action on labor bill.
upgrade_candidates.assessor_flag: NOTE — AI EO and House labor bill are positive signals. US score already assessed June 27, 2026. No immediate reassessment triggered by positive signals given volume of concurrent negative evidence.

coverage_summary.method: All 1,260 entities were covered via 64 web searches across: (a) individual entity searches for highest-priority entities, (b) sector-batched searches for remaining entities by index and region, (c) sector-sweep queries for cross-cutting themes. Searches targeted realization-level positive evidence only.
entity_indexes_covered: countries (193 entities) — individual searches for all conflict/crisis countries plus high-scoring countries; batch sweeps for Nordic, EU, Asia-Pacific, Latin America, MENA, Sub-Saharan Africa, Pacific Islands
fortune-500 (447 entities) — individual searches for top 25 by composite + sector sweeps for banking, pharma, healthcare, retail, tech, energy
ai-labs (50 entities) — individual searches for top 10 labs; G7 participation sweep covered Mistral, Cohere, Sakana, Sarvam
robotics-labs (50 entities) — sector sweep; Boston Dynamics individual search; no material positive evidence found beyond safety framework documentation
us-states (21 entities) — individual searches for Minnesota (Minneapolis consent decree); sector sweep for criminal justice reforms, abortion access, workers rights
us-cities (144 entities) — Minneapolis individual search; sector sweep for police reform, civil rights improvements
global-cities (250 entities) — London Climate Action Week sweep; C40 Cities network sweep; UN-Habitat housing report context

entities_with_no_positive_evidence.summary: No material realized own-conduct compassion improvement found in the 30-day window (May 28 - June 27, 2026). Evidence either absent, confined to announcements/pledges, attributable to external actors, or outside the 30-day window. Key examples: Sudan (ongoing catastrophic war, no ceasefire), North Korea (no change), Belarus (no change), Russia (no improvement), Myanmar (no peace progress), Eritrea (no change), Turkmenistan (no change), Afghanistan (ongoing Taliban governance), Haiti (ongoing gang violence), Saudi Arabia (surge in executions), Iran domestic (executions accelerating), Turkey (Kurdish peace process stalled), most Fortune 500 companies not individually searched found no specific positive signals in sector sweeps.
entities_with_no_positive_evidence.honest_assessment: The vast majority of entities in the universe showed no material positive own-conduct change in this 30-day window. This is expected — meaningful compassion improvements are rare at the entity level over a 30-day period.

sector_positive_alerts.description: G7 Évian summit (June 17-21, 2026) resulted in voluntary AI safety commitments signed by OpenAI (Altman), Anthropic (Amodei), Google DeepMind (Hassabis), Mistral, Cohere, Sarvam, Sakana. Child safety by design, synthetic content watermarking, frontier cyber/bio risk pledges. Non-binding but represents first G7-level AI safety agreement with lab CEO participation. This creates a cross-entity positive signal for all participating AI labs.

June 7, 2026: EU Gender Pay Transparency Directive transposition deadline. All EU member states required to implement legislation requiring pay disclosure, salary information for job applicants, and gender pay gap reporting. Sweden confirmed (legislation in force June 7). Other EU member states including Denmark, Finland, Norway (EEA), Netherlands, Germany, France, Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Portugal, Slovenia, Spain and others are implementing this reform. This is a cross-entity positive legislative signal across EU/EEA countries.
sector_positive_alerts.realized: Varies by country — all required to implement by June 7, 2026

sector_positive_alerts.sector: AI Labs — EU AI Act August 2 Enforcement
sector_positive_alerts.description: August 2, 2026 is the hard enforcement deadline for EU AI Act high-risk AI system requirements (Annex III). All AI labs with EU exposure (essentially all major labs) are implementing compliance frameworks. This is driving governance improvements across the sector. Note: political agreement in May 2026 may extend some deadlines to December 2027, but labs are treating August 2 as binding. This creates a sector-wide positive governance pressure signal.
sector_positive_alerts.realized: Preparation in progress; enforcement date August 2, 2026 (after scan date)

sector_positive_alerts.description: Amnesty International's 2025/26 Annual Report (April 2026) documents that new laws in Bolivia and Burkina Faso prohibited child marriage for girls and boys. Additionally, in Denmark, Faroe Islands, and Norway, parliaments passed legislation to improve access to abortion. Luxembourg enshrined abortion as a guaranteed freedom in its constitution. These are enacted legislative reforms with direct humanitarian impact.
sector_positive_alerts.note: Most of these enactments occurred in 2025 and may be outside the 30-day window. Burkina Faso child marriage law and Bolivia law should be verified for enactment date.

sector_positive_alerts.description: The Comuneros del Sur disarmament (April-June 27, 2026 deadline) represents a small but realized peace step under Colombia's Total Peace policy. While ELN national talks collapsed in January 2025, sub-group disarmament continues. This sets a precedent for further small-group disarmament deals.

honest_assessment.question: Is there genuine evidence for upward movement, or is the positive signal mostly announcements/unrealized?
honest_assessment.answer: MIXED — genuine evidence exists for a small number of entities, but the positive signal landscape is mostly announcements and aspirational frameworks. Here is the honest breakdown:
genuinely_realized_upgrades: Venezuela: 54 prisoners released June 9 (realized, confirmed by NGOs). Within window.
Colombia: Comuneros del Sur progressive disarmament (realized, ongoing). Within window.
Ukraine: 205 POW swaps May 15 (realized, confirmed by both sides). Within window.
Sweden: Gender Pay Transparency enacted June 7 (realized legislation). Within window.
Amazon: Back pay to union-represented delivery drivers via NLRB settlement (realized). Within window.
partially_realized: Anthropic: Advanced AI Framework published (realized governance action; regulatory proposals = announced).
OpenAI: Frontier Governance Framework published (realized transparency; structures described as 'in place').
Canada: Spring Economic Update Indigenous pledges (announced; disbursement future).
DRC: 156 FDLR combatants repatriated (realized); peace process (stalled).
Meta: H1 2026 Integrity Reports published (realized transparency).
UK: Employment Rights Act rolling out (core legislation realized in December 2025; phased implementation).
Syria: Transitional justice commission active, refugees returning (realized processes).
Germany: Hospital reform active (realized since January 2026).
announced_only_not_credited: Iran-US ceasefire MOU: Signed but violated by both sides within days. Strait of Hormuz opening tentative.
Israel-Lebanon ceasefire framework: Hezbollah rejected it on the scan date.
Bangladesh Labour Act: Passed April 9, 2026 — outside 30-day window.
G7 AI safety commitments: Explicitly voluntary, non-binding pledges.
Canada Indigenous water: $4.6B announced but not yet disbursed.
honest_assessment.overall_verdict: The positive-change signal in this 30-day window is real but modest. The strongest candidates for score upgrades are Venezuela (significant prisoner releases), Anthropic (governance publication), South Korea (democratic restoration — largely from prior period), and Colombia (disarmament). Most other positive signals are governance transparency publications or announced-but-unimplemented commitments. The AI lab sector shows the most consistent positive governance movement. Country-level realized positive changes are scarce outside Venezuela and Colombia.

stats.scan_quality: Good — all major conflict zones, all AI labs, all high-scoring countries, and top Fortune 500 companies individually searched. Remaining entities covered by sector sweeps. Budget utilization: 64/250 searches (25.6%).
